title: hypericum humifusum var. radicans
intro: Hypericum humifusum var. radicans, also known as creeping St. John's Wort, is a perennial plant species in the St. John's Wort genus, characterized by its trailing stems and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its content of hypericin and hyperforin. The health benefits of Hypericum humifusum var. radicans include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe wounds, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
